topic: Sharp uniform-in-DGP non-equivalence theorem between three leading double machine learning (DML) estimators for the average treatment effect under unconfoundedness: the original DML cross-fit estimator (Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins 2018, Econometrics Journal), the locally-robust orthogonal-score (LRO) estimator (Chernozhukov-Escanciano-Ichimura-Newey-Robins 2022, Econometrica), and the automatic debiased ML estimator (Chernozhukov-Newey-Singh 2022, Econometrica). All three target the same population functional E[Y(1)-Y(0)] and have first-order-equivalent influence functions when nuisance functions are estimated at the standard L^2 rate o(n^{-1/4}). The flagship question (axis b: non-equivalence frontier between three named published estimators): characterize the sharp boundary in nuisance-misspecification rate space r_g = r_g(n) for the outcome regression and r_p = r_p(n) for the propensity at which DML, LRO, and ADML have asymptotically equivalent variance vs. strictly different uniform-in-DGP variance lower bounds. The kernel claim is a closed-form spectral non-equivalence theorem: the three estimators achieve identical asymptotic variance bounds (and thus identical Wald confidence intervals at first order) if and only if r_g + r_p > rho_star, where rho_star is a sharp threshold function of the smallest eigenvalue of an explicit observable covariance kernel formed from the outcome residual and the propensity Riesz representer. When r_g + r_p <= rho_star, LRO and ADML strictly dominate DML by orthogonality, but LRO and ADML themselves diverge in variance lower bound proportional to the spectral gap, with ADML achieving the global semiparametric efficiency bound and LRO achieving only a local one. rho_star is a NEW mathematical object computable from observable second moments, not an existence/absence statement from prior work. Recovers the textbook L^2-rate equivalence (Chernozhukov et al 2018) at the boundary r_g+r_p=O(n^{-1/4}) and provides the first sharp triple non-equivalence boundary for DML estimators in closed form, separating the regime where DML, LRO, ADML give identical inference from the regime where the ordering DML < LRO < ADML is strict and quantifiable.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp uniform-in-DGP boundary at which the original cross-fit DML ATE estimator, LRO, and ADML have the same first-order law versus distinct variance lower bounds?

source_refs.claim: DML gives cross-fitted orthogonal-score ATE inference with uniform validity under nuisance-rate and overlap conditions.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: LRO constructs locally robust semiparametric moments by adding first-step influence adjustments and studies robustness of the resulting estimators.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: ADML debiases regression functionals automatically using the function of interest, Riesz representation, and misspecification-robust standard errors.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.claim: Regularized Riesz representers give orthogonal scores for global and local functionals and include ATE-adjacent debiased ML constructions.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.where: §8 Main results, Conjecture quotient-frontier; §11 Honest scope flags
source_refs.excerpt: BJS and estimator m are influence-function equivalent for theta_a if and only if Gamma_m(pi,Sigma,a)tau=0.
well-posedness flags (K_star=0 corner case undefined, Z_M projection loss not uniquely specified

open_problems.why_unsolved: The DML, LRO, ADML, and regularized-Riesz papers establish validity and efficiency properties for their own constructions, and sometimes pointwise equivalence under correct specification or standard rates. They do not give a necessary-and-sufficient uniform-in-DGP comparison of the three named estimators over nuisance-rate, overlap, and Riesz-approximation regimes. The closest prior ThmSmith estimator-frontier proposal was rejected partly because the estimator-specific projection/loadings were not pinned down, so the unresolved obstacle is a common normalization in which pairwise influence-function gaps are explicit and sharp rather than assumed.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp iff theorem over a stated DGP and learner class giving exactly when sup_P sqrt(n) pairwise estimator differences vanish, with matching local DGP sequences proving strict variance gaps outside the boundary.

open_problems.open_problem: Which locally robust semiparametric GMM moments, Jacobian normalizations, and weights reproduce the canonical ATE influence function used by DML and ADML, and which produce first-order distinct estimators?

source_refs.claim: LRO is a general semiparametric GMM construction, not a classification of when its moments collapse to a particular ATE score.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: The DML ATE construction uses an efficient orthogonal score for the unconfoundedness model.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.claim: ADML builds the bias correction from the regression functional and learned Riesz representer rather than requiring a hand-coded score.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.excerpt: the canonical gradient belongs to the parameter/model
define Gamma_m as the gap between each named estimator IF and the true efficient EIF

open_problems.why_unsolved: LRO deliberately covers a broad class of moments, while DML and ADML specialize to ATE or regression-functional scores. Published results do not classify the invariant score and normalization choices under which these routes produce the same ATE asymptotic linear representation. The prior reviewer warning is directly relevant: the efficient influence function belongs to the estimand and model, so the unresolved task is to compare estimator influence functions against one fixed EIF rather than to assign different canonical gradients to different estimators.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An equivalence classification theorem defining EIF_ATE once, expressing IF_DML - EIF_ATE, IF_LRO - EIF_ATE, and IF_ADML - EIF_ATE, and giving counterexamples for LRO choices outside the invariant class.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp joint nuisance-perturbation region in outcome-regression and propensity-score error space where DML, LRO, and ADML share validity, separate, or fail under misspecification?

source_refs.claim: The paper extends valid uniform ATE inference to cases where either the propensity or outcome estimator need not converge to truth.
source_refs.author: Avagyan, Vansteelandt

source_refs.claim: The paper shows doubly robust DML inference in semiparametric regression but notes nonregularity can occur when only one nuisance is consistent.
source_refs.author: Dukes, Vansteelandt, Whitney

source_refs.claim: DML removes first-order regularization and overfitting bias when nuisance predictions satisfy product-rate conditions.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins

source_refs.excerpt: the two worst-case mechanisms compete for the same response types
give a fully numeric two-action, three-reward witness with explicit budgets and strict inequalities

open_problems.why_unsolved: Rate-double-robust and misspecification-robust inference papers handle particular estimators or semiparametric regression models, but they do not compare whether the original DML, LRO, and ADML ATE estimators have the same boundary when one nuisance is inconsistent, both nuisances are misspecified, or their errors are jointly constrained. The prior partial-ID proposal flags the same mathematical obstruction in a different setting: rectangular one-nuisance envelopes can miss joint compatibility because the worst-case mechanisms compete for the same latent or response structure.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp partition theorem for joint (r_g, r_p, misspecification-bias) regimes into common-equivalence, common-validity, estimator-specific-validity, and common-failure regions, with finite-support lower-bound witnesses.

open_problems.open_problem: Can strict DML-LRO-ADML variance separation be derived from primitive finite-support DGP conditions rather than assumed as a positive spectral or margin condition?

source_refs.excerpt: Conjecture 1 asks to prove Assumption ref{ass:quant-margin} from the primitive fourth-cumulant geometry
the current theorem is not false, but the flagship claim has been demoted into an assumption
State a minimal three-period, two-cohort worked algebraic sub-claim
Add a concrete three-period, two-cohort worked matrix for Gamma_m

open_problems.why_unsolved: Two prior ThmSmith runs identify a recurring failure mode for non-equivalence proposals: the strict gap is placed into an assumption or left as an abstract operator condition, and reviewers ask for a minimal worked witness proving non-vacuity. For the DML topic, this means a proposed spectral rho_star or variance gap is not enough unless primitive probability, outcome, and treatment-assignment conditions imply the relevant eigenvalue or influence-function separation.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-support witness theorem giving explicit cell probabilities, nuisance perturbations, and estimator influence functions that imply a strictly positive pairwise variance gap, plus a primitive open-set extension around the witness.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the local-to-weak-overlap boundary where DML, LRO, and ADML point estimates remain equivalent but their variance estimators, studentization, or confidence intervals separate uniformly?

source_refs.claim: The regularized-Riesz framework emphasizes instability and nonregularity risks when Riesz representers are hard to estimate, including ATE-like inverse-propensity behavior.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.claim: ADML provides misspecification-robust standard errors for debiased regression-function estimators but does not compare studentized ATE laws across DML/LRO/ADML under weak overlap.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh

source_refs.claim: LRO reduces first-step sensitivity through locally robust moments, but robustness of standard errors depends on moment and parameter structure.
source_refs.author: Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, Robins

open_problems.why_unsolved: Weak overlap is a concrete obstacle because inverse propensity and Riesz-representer components can grow even when the target ATE remains formally identified. The cited estimator papers provide valid inference conditions and robust standard-error constructions, but they do not give a local asymptotic experiment that compares the three studentized statistics as propensity scores approach zero or one. Thus point-estimator equivalence may not imply variance-estimator or Wald-interval equivalence uniformly over local-to-weak-overlap DGPs.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A local asymptotic experiment for propensity scores approaching 0 or 1 that gives matching upper and lower bounds for point-estimator differences and studentized-statistic differences across DML, LRO, and ADML.

literature_map:
Chernozhukov et al. 2018, The Econometrics Journal, is the baseline DML ATE source: cross-fitting plus Neyman orthogonal scores gives uniform treatment-effect inference under product-rate and overlap conditions.
Chernozhukov, Escanciano, Ichimura, Newey, and Robins 2022, Econometrica, gives the locally robust semiparametric GMM construction and the main general LRO comparator.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh 2022, Econometrica, gives ADML for causal and structural regression effects using automatic bias correction and robust standard errors.
Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh 2022, The Econometrics Journal, gives regularized Riesz representers for global and local parameters, the closest paper to direct Riesz-versus-propensity comparisons.
Newey and Robins 2018 studies cross-fitting and fast remainder rates, relevant to when fold/sample-splitting choices preserve first-order equivalence.
Avagyan and Vansteelandt 2022 extends uniform ATE inference to cases where one nuisance estimator need not converge to truth.
Dukes, Vansteelandt, and Whitney 2024 develops doubly robust inference for DML in semiparametric regression and explicitly notes possible nonregularity when only one nuisance is consistent.
Bruns-Smith, Dukes, Feller, and Ogburn 2025 connects augmented balancing weights, regression, and AutoDML-style estimators through exact algebraic equivalences and tuning-dependent behavior.
The main tension is that each estimator family has validity or equivalence statements in its own regime, but the literature does not classify uniform pairwise non-equivalence among DML, LRO, and ADML.
The strongest open substrate is exact-ID semiparametric ATE: nuisance-rate boundaries, EIF normalization, joint misspecification geometry, and weak-overlap studentization are all live gaps.
prior_proposal_map:
Active ThmSmith/doc/formalization/eid_dml_orth_uniform contains only state/pipeline JSON and no proposal or review files yet.
The closest mined prior is ThmSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/failed/eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1: same axis-b structure, named estimators, common causal estimand, influence-function/equivalence frontier, and reviewer demands for explicit estimator matrices and EIF-baseline normalization.
The key reviewer JSON is eid_bjs_efficient_event_study_v1_reviews/angle2_v5.json, whose strengthening_paths request a Gamma_m gap to the true efficient EIF and a concrete worked matrix.
A second analogue is ThmSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/downgraded/eid_lp_svar_nonequiv_v1: a non-equivalence proposal downgraded because the strict generic margin was assumed rather than proved.
A third adjacent source is ThmSmith/doc/formalization/_bank/downgraded/pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: joint propensity/outcome misspecification and non-rectangularity, relevant to DML nuisance-perturbation geometry.
